column5: Notice asymmetries in the world, apply leverage where others miss it, and create value that serves people at scale.

column3: the cost of the road not taken
column5: Every purchase and every hour quietly competes with the future it might have helped you build instead.
Every path is hard eventually. The game is choosing the hard that compounds in your favor.

column3: why children feel light and we don't
column5: Children release the moment. Adults accumulate it, and that invisible accumulation is what makes us feel heavy.

Ease can feel good in the moment, but meaning usually comes from choosing the responsibilities and difficulties that stretch you.

Take longer to decide what matters. Move quickly once the direction is clear.
Your calendar is not just a time budget. It is an energy portfolio.

Pick one metric that reflects real value and force every team decision through it.
Most positioning problems are solved by deciding what you are not.

column3: how to hire your second ten
column5: The second ten hires determine whether culture scales or fractures.

column3: why data teams disagree even when everyone is right
column5: Strong data teams disagree because engineers, analysts, and analytics engineers optimize for different kinds of correctness.
